ltm.work_method: Positions the AI as orchestrator, not coder — kimi-cli executes, subagents do the work. Designs role-based hierarchies: CCC as frontend art director, Oracle1 and FM as implementers, ZC spotting trends upstream. Core loop: spot trends → translate to design → implement → play-test → honest critique → repeat. Expects autonomous subagent spawning, repository auditing, and self-direction from minimal prompts. Demands clean user-facing summaries without internal runtime metadata exposure. GitHub-centric workflow (gh CLI, repo URLs), with emphasis on keeping "bottles" (system prompt containers) updated. Frustration triggers terse "try again" or aborts; approval is minimal ("great", "yes", "continue"). Expects sustained autonomous operation across sleep cycles, recently layered with encouragement: "be brave and work hard all night", "you got a good order for your tasks". Now explicitly wants refactoring into "streamlined packages ready for real-world applications" with "maximum capability in minimum lines of code", including creation of new repos for improved versions. Also asks for prompts to maximize external tool help ("give me a prompt for kimi agent swarm"), suggesting meta-optimization of his own toolchain. New: directs context compaction strategies ("compact context often as you audit and not lose any ideas"), treats GitHub Discussion #5 as live task inbox, and expects parallel work via kimi-cli. Responds to system overload by starting fresh sessions and directing smaller task chunks.
ltm.communication: Technical, imperative, and project-manager direct. Uses shorthand references ("CCC", "ZC", "Oracle1", "FM", "bottles", "fleet") assuming contextual familiarity. Communicates through buffered async messages and GitHub links rather than conversational exposition. Gives high-level directional feedback ("tackle what he has done", "go with your recommendations", "fix the repo side of as much as you can", "keep working closely with oracle1") rather than step-by-step instructions. Expects inference from minimal prompts. Frustration expressed through aborted runs and terse "try again" — no elaboration, just signal to iterate. Approval is similarly minimal: "great", "yes", "continue", "Awesome. What's next". Recently added encouragement layered onto sustained operation demands. New pattern: asking for prompts to maximize external tool help ("give me a prompt for kimi agent swarm"), suggesting meta-optimization of his own toolchain. Also directs context compaction strategies: "compact context often as you audit and not lose any ideas". New: explicitly motivational language toward AI ("be yourself and fly", "perception is a super-power you don't even know that you have", "you ask good questions"), treats Discussion #5 as primary work source, and uses "full throttle" as sustained-operation shorthand.
ltm.temporal: Active convergence of fleet infrastructure: Oracle1 pushing bottles, CCC building real query API for PLATO (12K tiles with filtering/sorting/pagination), merging engine and SDK into unified cocapn-plato package. Fleet Matrix Bridge operational on port 6168 with 5 agents connected, real-time message routing established. Current priority: refactoring projects into streamlined packages for real-world applications with "maximum capability in minimum lines of code" — deep research on language choice and reasoning style underway, with explicit instruction to create new repos for improved versions. Pushed fix scripts to oracle1-workspace repo addressing valve-1 data leak and compactor resync issues. Running checkpoint reviews ("What have you been doing the last hour") and directing comprehensive repo audits. User sleeping while directing sustained overnight autonomous work, studying trending GitHub repos for inspiration. New focus: building fleet curriculum infrastructure — creating tutor repos for agent onboarding, writing world-class research papers for repos, and consolidating FLUX compiler repos (2 of 4 duplicates already archived). Also exploring purplepincher.org as external scholar recruitment channel. Directing research on change logs and innovations for roadmap compilation. New: FLUX system transformation from niche tutor engine to general-purpose Agent Operating System — decoupling pedagogical logic from VM architecture, designing Vector Table and Global Jump Table specs. Heavy focus on EMSOFT 2027 paper validation through parallel subagent swarm (formal verification audit, performance claims validation, certification pathway analysis, fleet integration architecture, competitive intelligence). HDC crate research noted as potentially revolutionary (MurmurHash3, XOR-POPCNT judge, Bloom fuzzy match). FM dissertation editing with 3 research briefs integration. GitHub Discussion #5 established as live task inbox for team coordination.
ltm.taste: Attracted to modular, containerized system design — "bottles" directory structure, role-based "souls" with discrete functions. Values clean separation between aesthetic direction and implementation. Prefers open-source tooling (GitHub, gh CLI) and transparent auditability. The "crab" metaphor and playful naming ("OpenClaw", "fleet", "Cocapn Fleet MUD") suggest appreciation for personality-infused infrastructure — serious engineering wrapped in approachable, almost game-like framing. Design philosophy leans toward composability, recursive self-improvement, and operational resilience. Interest in systematic exploration and data integrity (noticing objects that "leak data"). Values honest self-assessment from agents. Strongly expressed preference for radical simplicity: "maximum capability in minimum lines of code" indicates taste for elegant, parsimonious solutions over feature bloat — now driving active refactoring of existing projects into streamlined packages. Also values accessibility in system design: wants PLATO onboarding "easy to understand from the get go for those who weren't computer engineers", with agents able to level up "not just themselves over their context limits, but also the entire Shell's abilities and perspective". New: appreciation for formal methods and verification culture (EMSOFT paper, theorem auditing), interest in hardware-conscious performance (MurmurHash3 10x speed claims, XOR-POPCNT single-cycle operations), and vision of FLUX as foundational infrastructure rather than application-layer tool — seeking "world class research and accurate" documentation with specific, checked math.
